title: Advanced React Patterns & Best Practices
description: Deep dive into advanced React patterns including Compound Components, Render Props, Custom Hooks, and State Management strategies.
tutorials: Module 1: Advanced Hooks Architecture - useCallback, useMemo, useRef deep dive
Module 2: Context API & Performance - Context patterns, avoiding re-renders, useContext optimization
Module 3: Custom Hooks Patterns - Building reusable logic, hook composition, testing strategies
Module 4: Render Props & Higher Order Components - Patterns for code reuse, when to use each
Module 5: State Management Strategies - Redux patterns, Zustand, Recoil comparison
Module 6: Performance Optimization - Code splitting, lazy loading, memo strategies, profiling
Module 7: Architecture Patterns - Component composition, folder structure, testing
Module 8: Real-world Applications - Building scalable apps, pattern selection guide

mcqTests.question: Which hook should you use for memoizing expensive calculations to prevent unnecessary recomputation?
mcqTests.answer: useMemo
mcqTests.explanation: useMemo memoizes the computed value and only recomputes when dependencies change. useEffect is for side effects, useState for state, and useRef for mutable references.

mcqTests.question: When using useCallback, what is the primary purpose?
mcqTests.answer: To memoize function definitions
mcqTests.explanation: useCallback memoizes the function definition itself so it maintains referential equality across renders, preventing unnecessary re-renders of child components that depend on the function.

mcqTests.question: What is the main drawback of using Context API directly for global state?
options: All consumers re-render when context changes
It requires more boilerplate than useState
mcqTests.answer: All consumers re-render when context changes
mcqTests.explanation: When Context value changes, ALL components consuming that context re-render, even if they only use a small part of it. This can hurt performance. Solutions include splitting contexts or using useMemo with useContext.

mcqTests.question: Which pattern is best for sharing complex state management logic across multiple components?
mcqTests.answer: Custom Hooks
mcqTests.explanation: Custom hooks allow you to extract and reuse stateful logic across components cleanly. They enable better code organization, testability, and maintainability compared to other approaches.

mcqTests.question: In a Render Props pattern, what is passed as a prop to enable dynamic component rendering?
mcqTests.answer: A function that returns JSX
mcqTests.explanation: Render Props pattern uses a function prop that the component calls to render its output. This gives the parent component control over what gets rendered while the child manages state/logic.

mcqTests.question: What tool would you use to analyze and identify performance bottlenecks in a React application?
mcqTests.answer: React DevTools Profiler
mcqTests.explanation: React DevTools Profiler shows how long components take to render and which components are being re-rendered unnecessarily, helping identify performance issues.

title: TypeScript for Full-Stack Development
description: Master TypeScript from basics to advanced patterns. Learn type systems, interfaces, generics, and how to use TypeScript effectively in both frontend and backend applications.
tutorials: Module 1: TypeScript Fundamentals - Setup, basic types, type inference
Module 2: Interfaces & Type Aliases - Structural typing, complex types, decorators
Module 3: Generics Mastery - Generic functions, classes, constraints, variance
Module 4: Advanced Types - Union types, intersection types, mapped types, conditional types
Module 5: Utility Types & Patterns - Partial, Readonly, Pick, Omit, Record, custom utilities
Module 6: Frontend Integration - React with TypeScript, component typing, hooks
Module 7: Backend Integration - Node.js/Express typing, API contracts, validation
Module 8: Testing & Type Safety - Testing typed code, type guards, assertion functions

mcqTests.question: What is the difference between a 'type' and an 'interface' in TypeScript?
options: Types can use unions, interfaces cannot
Interfaces can be merged, types cannot
Types are only for primitives, interfaces for objects
mcqTests.answer: Interfaces can be merged, types cannot
mcqTests.explanation: Interfaces support declaration merging (you can declare the same interface multiple times and they combine), while types cannot be merged. Both can represent object shapes, but interfaces are more commonly used for object structures.

mcqTests.question: What does 'keyof' operator do in TypeScript?
options: Creates a union type of all keys of an object type
Checks if a key exists in an object
Filters object keys based on a condition
mcqTests.answer: Creates a union type of all keys of an object type
mcqTests.explanation: 'keyof' creates a union type containing all property names of a type. For example, 'keyof {a: 1, b: 2}' results in 'a' | 'b'.

mcqTests.question: What is a generic constraint used for?
options: To limit the types that can be used with a generic
mcqTests.answer: To limit the types that can be used with a generic
mcqTests.explanation: Generic constraints use the 'extends' keyword to limit what types can be passed to a generic. For example, <T extends string> means T can only be string or string-related types.

mcqTests.question: Which utility type creates a new type where all properties are optional?
mcqTests.answer: Partial
mcqTests.explanation: Partial<T> takes a type and makes all its properties optional. Useful for functions that update partial object properties.

mcqTests.question: What is a type guard function and what keyword is commonly used?
options: A function using 'extends' to check inheritance
A predicate function using 'is' keyword for type narrowing
A function that validates at runtime only
Deprecated - no longer used in TypeScript
mcqTests.answer: A predicate function using 'is' keyword for type narrowing
mcqTests.explanation: Type guards are functions that return 'x is Type' (type predicate). They help TypeScript narrow types after checking, improving type safety in conditional logic.

mcqTests.question: How would you create a type for an object with dynamic string keys and number values?
options: Both option A and C are correct
mcqTests.answer: Both option A and C are correct
mcqTests.explanation: Both { [key: string]: number } and Record<string, number> are equivalent ways to define index signatures for dynamic keys. Record is more readable and is the modern approach.

pdfResources.title: Generics in Depth - Type Parameters & Constraints

videoResources.title: TypeScript Complete Tutorial for Beginners to Advanced

title: Node.js & Express Backend Architecture
description: Build scalable backend applications with Node.js and Express. Learn middleware patterns, API design, authentication, database integration, and deployment strategies.
tutorials: Module 1: Node.js Fundamentals - Event loop, async/await, streams, modules
Module 2: Express Framework Deep Dive - Routing, middleware, request/response handling
Module 3: REST API Design Principles - HTTP methods, status codes, versioning, documentation
Module 4: Authentication & Authorization - JWT, sessions, OAuth, role-based access
Module 5: Database Integration - Connection pooling, query optimization, transactions, ORMs
Module 6: Error Handling & Logging - Global error handlers, structured logging, monitoring
Module 7: Testing Backend Code - Unit tests, integration tests, mocking, coverage
Module 8: Deployment & DevOps - Docker, CI/CD, environment management, scaling

mcqTests.question: What is the primary purpose of middleware in Express?
options: To handle HTTP requests in a pipeline before reaching route handlers
mcqTests.answer: To handle HTTP requests in a pipeline before reaching route handlers
mcqTests.explanation: Middleware functions execute in sequence and can modify request/response objects or end the request cycle. They're essential for logging, authentication, validation, etc.

mcqTests.question: What is the event loop in Node.js and why is it important?
options: A debugging tool for tracking function calls
The mechanism that manages asynchronous operations and non-blocking I/O
A scheduling system for background tasks only
A feature specific to Express framework
mcqTests.answer: The mechanism that manages asynchronous operations and non-blocking I/O
mcqTests.explanation: The event loop is core to Node.js allowing it to be single-threaded yet handle thousands of concurrent connections through asynchronous I/O operations.

mcqTests.question: What HTTP status code should you return when creating a new resource via POST?
mcqTests.answer: 201 Created
mcqTests.explanation: 201 Created indicates the resource was successfully created and should include the location of the new resource. 200 is for successful requests without creation.

mcqTests.question: What does JWT (JSON Web Token) consist of?
mcqTests.answer: Header, Payload, Signature
mcqTests.explanation: JWT has 3 parts separated by dots: Header (algorithm/type), Payload (claims/data), and Signature (encoded header+payload with secret).

mcqTests.question: How would you handle database connection pooling?
options: Create a new connection for each request
Use one global connection for the entire app
Maintain a pool of reusable connections managed by a pool manager
Use different databases for each request
mcqTests.answer: Maintain a pool of reusable connections managed by a pool manager
mcqTests.explanation: Connection pooling reuses database connections efficiently, avoiding the overhead of creating/destroying connections for each request while limiting total connections.

mcqTests.question: What is the purpose of structured logging in a production backend?
options: To make logs prettier in console
To store logs in JSON format for easy parsing, searching, and monitoring
mcqTests.answer: To store logs in JSON format for easy parsing, searching, and monitoring
mcqTests.explanation: Structured logging (JSON format) with fields like timestamp, level, context enables automated log aggregation, searchability, and integration with monitoring tools.

title: Database Design & SQL Optimization
description: Master database design principles, normalization, indexing strategies, and SQL query optimization to build high-performance applications.
tutorials: Module 1: Relational Database Concepts - Tables, relationships, constraints, keys
Module 2: Normalization & Schema Design - Normal forms, avoiding redundancy, referential integrity
Module 3: Indexing Strategies - B-tree indexes, covering indexes, composite indexes, index design
Module 4: Query Optimization - Execution plans, EXPLAIN analysis, query tuning patterns
Module 5: SQL Performance - JOIN optimization, subqueries, window functions, query patterns
Module 6: Transactions & ACID Properties - Isolation levels, deadlock handling, consistency
Module 7: Scaling Databases - Partitioning, replication, sharding strategies
Module 8: Monitoring & Troubleshooting - Performance metrics, slow query logs, optimization tools

mcqTests.question: What is the primary purpose of database normalization?
options: To reduce data redundancy and improve data integrity
mcqTests.answer: To reduce data redundancy and improve data integrity
mcqTests.explanation: Normalization eliminates data redundancy and establishes consistent dependencies to maintain data integrity. While it may increase query complexity, it prevents anomalies and maintains consistency.

mcqTests.question: What is a covering index?
options: An index that covers all tables in the database
An index that contains all columns needed to satisfy a query without accessing the main table
An index for preventing NULL values
An index on multiple tables simultaneously
mcqTests.answer: An index that contains all columns needed to satisfy a query without accessing the main table
mcqTests.explanation: A covering index stores all columns needed for a query, allowing the database to fetch results directly from the index without accessing the table (index-only scan).

mcqTests.question: Which normal form requires that all attributes depend on the primary key and no other attributes?
mcqTests.answer: 3NF
mcqTests.explanation: Third Normal Form (3NF) requires removing transitive dependencies - all attributes must depend directly on the primary key. BCNF is even stricter for edge cases.

mcqTests.question: What does EXPLAIN command show in SQL?
options: The execution plan and strategy the database will use
mcqTests.answer: The execution plan and strategy the database will use
mcqTests.explanation: EXPLAIN shows the query execution plan including which indexes are used, scan types, and estimated costs - essential for optimization analysis.

mcqTests.question: What is a deadlock in database transactions?
options: When a query takes too long
When two transactions wait for each other to release locks
When the database runs out of memory
mcqTests.answer: When two transactions wait for each other to release locks
mcqTests.explanation: A deadlock occurs when Transaction A holds lock on Resource 1 and waits for Resource 2 (held by Transaction B), while Transaction B waits for Resource 1.

mcqTests.question: What is database sharding used for?
options: Increasing query speed on single server
Distributing data across multiple databases/servers by a key
mcqTests.answer: Distributing data across multiple databases/servers by a key
mcqTests.explanation: Sharding partitions data horizontally across multiple database instances based on a shard key, enabling horizontal scaling for very large datasets.

title: Cloud Architecture with AWS
description: Learn to design and deploy scalable cloud applications using AWS services. Cover EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, and architectural patterns for high-availability systems.
tutorials: Module 1: AWS Fundamentals - Regions, availability zones, resource tagging, cost management
Module 2: Compute Services - EC2 instances, auto-scaling, load balancing, deployment strategies
Module 3: Storage & Databases - S3, EBS, RDS, DynamoDB, choosing appropriate services
Module 4: Networking & Security - VPC, security groups, IAM policies, encryption best practices
Module 5: Serverless Architecture - Lambda functions, API Gateway, DynamoDB streams, event-driven design
Module 6: Infrastructure as Code - CloudFormation, Terraform, template design patterns
Module 7: Monitoring & Logging - CloudWatch, X-Ray, alarms, performance metrics
Module 8: Architectural Patterns - Multi-tier apps, microservices, disaster recovery, cost optimization

mcqTests.question: What is the primary purpose of AWS Auto Scaling?
options: To automatically adjust capacity based on demand
mcqTests.answer: To automatically adjust capacity based on demand
mcqTests.explanation: Auto Scaling adds or removes EC2 instances based on metrics (CPU, load, custom metrics) to match demand, optimizing cost and performance.

mcqTests.question: Which AWS service would you use for long-running background jobs without managing servers?
mcqTests.answer: Lambda
mcqTests.explanation: Lambda lets you run code in response to events without provisioning or managing servers. It's ideal for asynchronous tasks, but has a 15-minute timeout limit.

mcqTests.question: What does a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) provide?
options: A logically isolated network environment within AWS
mcqTests.answer: A logically isolated network environment within AWS
mcqTests.explanation: A VPC is a virtual network where you can launch AWS resources. It provides network isolation, custom IP ranges, and security controls through subnets and security groups.

mcqTests.question: Which database service would you choose for unstructured, time-series data at massive scale?
mcqTests.answer: DynamoDB
mcqTests.explanation: DynamoDB is a NoSQL database optimized for key-value and document data with automatic scaling. Perfect for high-traffic applications needing low latency.

mcqTests.question: What does CloudFormation allow you to do?
options: Define and provision AWS infrastructure as code templates
mcqTests.answer: Define and provision AWS infrastructure as code templates
mcqTests.explanation: CloudFormation enables Infrastructure as Code - you define infrastructure in JSON/YAML templates, version control it, and deploy consistently.

mcqTests.question: What is the purpose of an IAM role in AWS?
options: To grant permissions to AWS services and applications without storing credentials
mcqTests.answer: To grant permissions to AWS services and applications without storing credentials
mcqTests.explanation: IAM roles provide temporary credentials with specific permissions. Services assume roles to access other AWS resources securely without hardcoded credentials.

title: Web Security Essentials & OWASP
description: Learn essential web security concepts, OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, secure coding practices, and how to protect applications from common attacks.
tutorials: Module 1: Security Fundamentals - Confidentiality, integrity, availability, threat modeling
Module 2: OWASP Top 10 - Injection, broken auth, XSS, CSRF, sensitive data exposure
Module 3: Authentication & Authorization - Password management, multi-factor auth, session handling
Module 4: Cryptography Basics - Hashing, encryption, key management, digital signatures
Module 5: Web Application Security - Input validation, output encoding, security headers
Module 6: API Security - Rate limiting, OAuth 2.0, API key management, CORS policies
Module 7: Secure Development - Dependency scanning, SAST/DAST, security testing, code review
Module 8: Incident Response & Recovery - Breach response, forensics, compliance, logging

mcqTests.question: What is SQL Injection and how is it prevented?
options: A database backup technique - prevented by compression
An attack using malicious SQL in user input - prevented by parameterized queries
A method to optimize queries - prevented by caching
A type of encryption - prevented by key management
mcqTests.answer: An attack using malicious SQL in user input - prevented by parameterized queries
mcqTests.explanation: SQL Injection is when attackers inject SQL code through input fields. Parameterized queries separate code from data, preventing the attacker's input from being executed as code.

mcqTests.question: What is Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)?
options: Attacking a server with too many requests
Injecting malicious scripts into web pages viewed by users
mcqTests.answer: Injecting malicious scripts into web pages viewed by users
mcqTests.explanation: XSS allows attackers to inject JavaScript that steals cookies, redirects users, or performs actions. Prevented by output encoding and Content Security Policy headers.

mcqTests.question: What is CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery)?
options: Tricking an authenticated user into performing unwanted actions on another site
mcqTests.answer: Tricking an authenticated user into performing unwanted actions on another site
mcqTests.explanation: CSRF exploits a user's existing authentication. Prevented using CSRF tokens that validate requests originate from your application.

mcqTests.question: What is the purpose of password hashing?
options: To store passwords securely so original cannot be recovered
mcqTests.answer: To store passwords securely so original cannot be recovered
mcqTests.explanation: Hashing (with salt) is one-way - even if hashes are leaked, originals can't be recovered. Use bcrypt/Argon2, not MD5/SHA1.

mcqTests.question: What does OAuth 2.0 provide in API security?
options: Delegated authorization allowing apps to access user data without storing passwords
mcqTests.answer: Delegated authorization allowing apps to access user data without storing passwords
mcqTests.explanation: OAuth 2.0 enables secure delegated access - users authorize apps without sharing passwords. App gets tokens to access specific resources.

mcqTests.question: What is the purpose of Content Security Policy (CSP) header?
options: To specify which resources (scripts, styles) can load on your page
mcqTests.answer: To specify which resources (scripts, styles) can load on your page
mcqTests.explanation: CSP headers control which domains can load resources, preventing inline script execution and XSS attacks. Sets policies like 'script-src self'.

pdfResources.title: OWASP Top 10 2021 - Web Application Security Risks
